methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Vertical Aerospace, a public-stage transportation company founded by Stephen Fitzpatrick, has raised over $400M to date. The broader transportation sector contains five private peers of relevance—Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft—though no direct public comparables exist in this subsector.

Peer-median valuation is not applicable here. The available peer set is too narrow; filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yields fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. A cross-sector median would introduce material distortion given the distinct capital intensity and development timelines within transportation verticals.

Without sufficient peer data, traditional comparable-company analysis cannot be executed with confidence. The analysis would benefit from either: expansion to adjacent transportation subsectors with deeper public market representation, or reliance on alternative methodologies such as DCF modeling or precedent transaction analysis within aviation or autonomous systems. Key uncertainties include the appropriate revenue multiple for pre-revenue or early-revenue stage transportation assets, and the discount rate applicable to frontier-tech execution risk. Until peer density improves or alternative valuation approaches are applied, a specific valuation range cannot be derived from the data currently available.
